topics.title: Ask for clarity (script)
topics.subtitle: A short script to get what you need without drama.

bridge_messages.INDIVIDUAL: Be specific: what decision, what by when, and what you tried.
bridge_messages.PEOPLE_LEADER: Reduce uncertainty: what changes, what stays, where to ask, and what to do next.

role_specific_framing.INDIVIDUAL: Use this when you feel stuck because you don’t know what’s expected.
role_specific_framing.PEOPLE_LEADER: Use this when your team is waiting because decision rights are unclear.

INDIVIDUAL: Ask what ‘good’ looks like this week.

INDIVIDUAL.label: One-sentence ask
INDIVIDUAL.text: Here’s what I need to move forward: [X]. Who owns this decision, and what’s the next step?

PEOPLE_LEADER.label: Manager reply
PEOPLE_LEADER.text: Here’s what we know, what we don’t, and what we’re doing this week. Questions go here: [channel].

topics.title: 10-minute huddle script
topics.subtitle: A simple structure to make the next step obvious.

bridge_messages.INDIVIDUAL: Tell your leader what blocked you and what you tried.
bridge_messages.PEOPLE_LEADER: Protect capacity: one standard, one example, one practice.

role_specific_framing.INDIVIDUAL: Use this to ask for a clear weekly standard and practice focus.
role_specific_framing.PEOPLE_LEADER: Use this to create a predictable weekly rhythm during change.

INDIVIDUAL: Ask what the weekly standard is.
Ask what one example ‘good’ looks like.
Ask what to practice this week.
PEOPLE_LEADER: State the standard in one sentence.
Pick one practice task and one blocker question.

INDIVIDUAL.label: Ask for the weekly standard
INDIVIDUAL.text: What is the one thing we should do consistently this week in the new way?

PEOPLE_LEADER.label: 10-minute huddle
PEOPLE_LEADER.text: This week’s expectation is: [one sentence]. Why it matters: [one sentence]. Today’s change: [one sentence]. Practice: [5 minutes]. Blockers: [2 minutes].
